items.rationale: His single most sustained thematic obsession — wants decentralized energy grids, transportation, wireless networks; frames the key test as 'verification cost < incumbent margin.' Repeatedly speaks at DePIN events and still probing primitives (decentralized DB access control) in 2024.
items.age_note: Theme runs continuously from Jan 2023 through Jan 2024; most recent touch 2024-01-26, so current.

citations.quote: We should have a decentralized energy grid, transportation network, and wireless network
The essential question when considering building a DePIN network: Verification cost < incumbent margin
very useful for a lot of DePIN companies, but doesn't exist as a neutral protocol primitive?

items.rationale: Most recent and emotionally explicit enthusiasm — calls restaking his 'ultimate nerdsnipe' and is openly optimistic about EigenDA and broad AVS deployment. He helped onboard the team to CSX.

citations.quote: Re-staking was the ultimate nerdsnipe for me, and I am very optimistic for the future presented by EigenDA and prolific AVS deployment.

items.rationale: Personal intellectual brand — wants to reframe mechanism design + cryptography for coordination beyond finance, sees tokens as 'value vectors' that expand the design space for redesigning societies. Reinforced by praising Kominers' token book in Jan 2024.
items.age_note: Core thesis from Apr 2023; re-engaged via 'The Everything Token' on 2024-01-26, so still live.

citations.quote: I would suggest we rebrand the study of using mechanism design and cryptography to solve coordination problems as “Tokenology.”
Tokens are value vectors. Dollars are value scalars. Tokens matter because they allow builders to keep the high dimensionality inherent in value representations

items.rationale: Explicitly believes the field under-invests in infrastructure relative to apps and sees building coordination rails as the larger opportunity.
items.age_note: Stated once on 2023-07-25 (~8 months old) and never restated, but consistent with his subsequent infra-heavy interests.

citations.quote: I actually think we don't have enough focus on crypto infrastructure relative to apps. Building the rails for the future of human coordination seems like a massive opportunity

items.rationale: Recurring technical fascination with verifiable computation and privacy — celebrates SNARK performance breakthroughs (JOLT/Lasso) and privacy on Ethereum (Aztec). Views decentralized verification as the crux of why crypto matters.
items.age_note: Heaviest activity mid-2023; latest related touch is admiring a cypherpunk ZK manifesto 2024-03-15.

citations.quote: Shocking work to wrangle SNARK performance forward. Another must read banger from @SuccinctJT!
Decentralized verification is the hardest problem and crux of why crypto matters

items.rationale: Believes ML should be decentralized — interested in storing LLMs on Filecoin/Arweave, proving inference with validity proofs, and DAO-owned model protocols. Backed Gensyn.
items.age_note: Concentrated in early-to-mid 2023; not restated in 2024 but never reversed (persists).

citations.quote: We're thrilled to be working with @harrygrieve, @benfielding and the team at @gensynai. The future of machine learning is decentralized

items.rationale: Sustained enthusiasm for trustless lending primitives via long association with Morpho; framed Morpho Blue launch as a major DeFi milestone.

citations.quote: Congrats to @PaulFrambot and the whole @MorphoLabs team for launching Morpho Blue. One small step for Morpho, one big step for DeFi
Big day for trustless lending! It's been an honor to work with @PaulFrambot and the @MorphoLabs team

items.rationale: Excited by protocols/DAOs where anyone can contribute and be paid in proportion to value created; flags the open problem of evaluating contribution quality after the fact.
items.age_note: Stated Feb 2023 (~13 months old), never restated; held with an acknowledged unsolved verification problem.

citations.quote: I'm very excited about the idea of permissionless contribution.
the open question for permissionless contribution is how to evaluate the quality and value created of a contribution after the fact

items.rationale: Skeptical that networks marketed as decentralized actually are — pointedly accuses projects of concealing a multisig behind a 'decentralized' label.

citations.quote: You are hiding a multi-sig in that decentralized network, are you not?

items.rationale: Thinks 'MEV' conflates user-harming extraction with benign/helpful activity, and that the harmful, information-asymmetry-driven extraction is an unsolved design problem.
items.age_note: From May 2023 (~10 months old), not restated since.

citations.quote: MEV is a bad term because it includes both actions that hurt users, and actions that are neutral or helpful for users while assisting in protocol design

items.rationale: Sarcastically frames incumbents and regulators as fearing decentralized networks; dislikes American bureaucracy and sees power structures as resistant to disruption.
items.age_note: Anti-bureaucracy stance dates to 2022; restated as regulatory-capture concern 2023-10-26.

citations.quote: nooo babe I love your regulatory capture the decentralized networks scare me
I dislike American bureaucracy

items.rationale: Acknowledges decentralization is always less efficient than centralization, so projects must justify who bears that cost and why it's worth paying — a sober counterweight to his optimism.
items.age_note: Framing from Oct 2022 (~18 months old), foundational and never contradicted.

citations.quote: Decentralization is always less efficient than centralization. Why is this cost worth paying, and who will bear it?

items.rationale: His most recent and most explicitly optimistic protocol bet — restaking as 'ultimate nerdsnipe,' bullish on EigenDA and broad AVS deployment.

citations.quote: Re-staking was the ultimate nerdsnipe for me, and I am very optimistic for the future presented by EigenDA and prolific AVS deployment.

items.rationale: Long-running working relationship; views Morpho Blue as a leap forward for trustless lending.

citations.quote: launching Morpho Blue. One small step for Morpho, one big step for DeFi

items.rationale: Top-of-mind in the modular/DA stack; jokes that only Celestia counts as 'real' data availability, signaling positive attention.
items.age_note: Light signal (humorous), but most recent DA reference (2024-03-02).

citations.quote: If it's not from the Celestia region of France it's just sparkling data availability

items.rationale: Sees its committed capacity as a potential data-availability layer (FVM verifying proofs), pending latency/erasure-coding work.
items.age_note: From 2023-07-27 (~8 months old), posed as an open exploration, not restated.

citations.quote: Anyone experimenting with Filecoin as a data availability layer? The FVM could verify proofs in theory and Filecoin has massive amounts of committed capacity

items.rationale: Personally using the Solana Saga phone and soliciting native dapp recommendations — tactical, experiential interest rather than a deep thesis.

citations.quote: I'm in my Solana Saga arc. Give me recommendations for native dapps or great experiences on @solanamobile

risk_regime.rationale: Persistently constructive builder-optimist toward decentralized networks and crypto infrastructure across the full corpus — celebrates BUIDL energy, calls ETH Denver a 'whitepill,' and keeps deploying conviction into new infra (DePIN, restaking, ZK). His caution is selective and design-level (fake decentralization, MEV, efficiency cost), not a macro de-risking. No market-timing or bearish posture appears.

notes: Wuollet writes as an a16z infrastructure investor/builder, not a trader; he discusses protocols and design primitives far more than tradable tokens, so 'tokens_excited' maps his protocol enthusiasm and conviction is dampened where the signal is a working relationship or a joke rather than an investment thesis. Most recent (2024 Q1) and highest-conviction signals: EigenLayer/restaking, Morpho/DeFi, DePIN primitives, Celestia/DA. No stance reversals detected — his views accrete rather than flip; older items (permissionless contribution, MEV framing, efficiency-cost framing) are marked 'persisted' where stated once and never restated but never contradicted. Several entries lean on his consistent track record rather than a single fresh quote. Conviction scores reflect intensity/recency, not certainty of outcome.
